ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "date_reported" TEXT /* Date and time the incident was reported */,
  "event_number" TEXT /* Unique event number assigned to the incident */,
  "case_number" TEXT /* Case number associated with the incident */,
  "type_of_event" INTEGER /* Type of event (foreign key to event_types) */,
  "date_from" TEXT /* Start date and time of the incident */,
  "date_to" TEXT /* End date and time of the incident */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the incident occurred (foreign key to locations) */,
  "disposition" INTEGER /* Final disposition of the incident (foreign key to dispositions) */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the incident (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* User who reported the incident (foreign key to users) */,
  "response_team" TEXT /* Team or individual responsible for the initial response */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time it took to respond to the incident */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time it took to resolve the incident */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes about the incident */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident (e.g., Open, In Progress, Closed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_reported_by FOREIGN KEY ("reported_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_type_of_event FOREIGN KEY ("type_of_event") REFERENCES event_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_disposition FOREIGN KEY ("disposition") REFERENCES dispositions ("disposition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., On Campus, Non-campus) */,
  "contact_information" TEXT /* Contact information for the location (e.g., phone number, email) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "building_name" TEXT /* Name of the building if applicable */,
  "floor" TEXT /* Floor of the building if applicable */,
  "security_camera" INTEGER /* Indicates if there are security cameras at the location */,
  "accessibility" TEXT /* Indicates if the location is accessible (e.g., wheelchair accessible) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the event type */,
  "default_response_team" TEXT /* Default response team for the event type */,
  "required_equipment" TEXT /* Equipment required for responding to this type of event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dispositions" (
  "disposition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disposition */,
  "disposition_name" TEXT /* Name of the disposition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disposition */,
  "resolution_time_required" TEXT /* Expected time required to resolve the incident */,
  "follow_up_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if follow-up actions are required */,
  "follow_up_instructions" TEXT /* Instructions for any follow-up actions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disposition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., security personnel, administrator, student) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access the user has (e.g., Admin, Staff, Student) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the log entry */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time the log entry was made */,
  "log_entry" TEXT /* Details of the log entry */,
  "status_before" TEXT /* Status of the incident before the log entry */,
  "status_after" TEXT /* Status of the incident after the log entry */,
  "response_team" TEXT /* Team or individual who made the log entry */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time taken to make the log entry */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes about the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_media" (
  "media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media file */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the media file is associated with */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the media file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the media file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the media file was uploaded */,
  "media_type" TEXT /* Type of media (e.g., Photo, Video) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the media file */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* User who uploaded the media file */,
  "is_private" INTEGER /* Indicates if the media file is private (e.g., for internal use only) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_media_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_media_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)
